tools.name: glim_twitter_search
tools.description: Search Twitter/X. Returns a compact human-readable list by default; pass format='json' for full structured data. Use glim_twitter_get(ref) for full thread context. Use docs://search-operators for reference.

tools.name: glim_twitter_get
tools.description: Fetch a tweet or a user from one reference. A tweet URL (incl. handle-less /i/status/<id>) returns the tweet with full thread context, parent, and optional replies/quotes; a profile URL (https://x.com/<handle>) returns the user with recent tweets. Prefer full URLs - if you only have a numeric id, pa

tools.name: glim_reddit_search
tools.description: Search Reddit posts. Each result comes with full post content and its top comments, so a single search usually answers the question without follow-up. Compact human-readable text by default; pass format='json' for full structured data. Use glim_reddit_get(ref) for a single post's complete comment tr

tools.name: glim_reddit_get
tools.description: Fetch a Reddit post, subreddit, or user by ref. Posts return comments; subreddits and users return profile metadata plus recent activity.

tools.name: glim_web_search
tools.description: Semantic web search powered by Exa. Returns titles, URLs, and the top query-relevant excerpt per result. Compact text by default; pass format='json' for full structured data incl. all excerpts per result. Use glim_web_fetch(url) for full page content.

tools.name: glim_web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a single web page and extract clean content. Auto-tier server-side: handles SSR (Next.js, Nuxt, TikTok, Pinterest, YouTube), SPA shells, PDFs, paywall detection, residential-proxy escalation, and stealth profiles for TikTok / Instagram / Pinterest / YouTube. Returns clean markdown (default) wi

tools.name: glim_github_search
tools.description: Search GitHub repositories, conversations (issues+PRs), or code, with full GitHub search syntax in the query: qualifiers (repo:, org:/user:, language:, path:, symbol:, content:, is:, stars:, label:, sort:stars), boolean AND/OR/NOT with parentheses, "exact strings", and /regex/. kind='repos': MINIMAL

tools.name: glim_github_get
tools.description: Fetch GitHub data from a single ref. GitHub URL or 'owner/repo' shorthand. A repo URL or owner/repo returns metadata + README; /pull/N -> PR (with comments + changed files), /issues/N -> issue, /blob/<ref>/<path> -> file (raw.githubusercontent.com URLs work too), /tree/<ref>[/<path>] -> file tree (o

tools.name: glim_amazon_search
tools.description: Pass exactly ONE of {query} or {category_slug}. Searches Amazon (com|co.uk|de|fr|es|it) and returns ranked hits with buybox price (gross + VAT-excluded net), ratings, review counts, and ASINs. Drill down with glim_amazon_get(ref). Set sort_by='most_reviewed' (with min_reviews to filter junk) for a t

tools.name: glim_amazon_get
tools.description: Fetch Amazon product detail from a full product URL (the marketplace - com|co.uk|de|fr|es|it - is read from the URL host; pass the url field from a glim_amazon_search result, or any /dp/<ASIN> page URL). Returns title, buybox price (gross + VAT-excluded net), stock, delivery estimate, rating, top re

tools.name: glim_youtube_get
tools.description: Fetch a YouTube video transcript from a video URL or 11-char id. The transcript is cleaned server-side: deduplicated, tags/HTML stripped, with coarse [m:ss] timestamps - roughly a tenth the size of the raw captions. Default format='text' returns it inline (when it fits ~40K chars / ~10K tokens) so a

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: glim_twitter_search, glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_reddit_get, glim_web_search, glim_web_fetch, glim_github_search, glim_github_get, glim_amazon_search, glim_amazon_get, glim_youtube_get · Private data: glim_github_search, glim_github_get, glim_youtube_get · Public sink: glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_reddit_get, glim_web_fetch · Destructive: glim_twitter_search, glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_web_search, glim_github_search, glim_amazon_search, glim_amazon_get, glim_youtube_get.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: glim_twitter_search, glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_reddit_get, glim_web_search, glim_web_fetch, glim_github_search, glim_github_get, glim_amazon_search, glim_amazon_get, glim_youtube_get · Private data: glim_github_search, glim_github_get, glim_youtube_get · Public sink: glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_reddit_get, glim_web_fetch · Destructive: glim_twitter_search, glim_twitter_get, glim_reddit_search, glim_web_search, glim_github_search, glim_amazon_search, glim_amazon_get, glim_youtube_get

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
